summary: Q2 and Q3 are sufficiently answered for architecture without another research-loop benchmark: use a 15-minute source freshness cache, optional 5-minute exact HMAC prompt-result cache, 80-token default brief, 120-token hard cap, and reject 40-token-only output. The full 200-prompt corpus run belongs to the regression harness child.

Ranked implementation order is contract first, freshness/source cache second, renderer harness before runtime adapters, core producer before runtime wiring, Claude first as the user-visible slice, then Gemini/Copilot and Codex as parity expansions.

Go/no-go matrix marks 002-006 and 009 as must-ship, defers 007 Gemini/Copilot and 008 Codex from the first slice, and drops only rejected designs such as kind skill-advisor, similarity-only result cache, 40-token-only brief, and runtime-specific renderer text.

Validation ownership is explicit: 005 owns renderer fixtures, normalized runtime-output comparators, privacy assertions, token cap checks, and the 019/004 200-prompt parity gate; runtime children consume those fixtures rather than inventing adapter-specific advice text.

Cross-cutting risks are latency budget mismatch, advisor over-authority, runtime adapter drift, privacy leakage, shared-payload vocabulary churn, Codex hook volatility, current advisor degraded health, and parent doc drift around kind skill-advisor.

Remaining open questions are now follow-up validations rather than architecture blockers: full corpus/timing run, real runtime captures for Claude/Copilot/Codex, prompt lifecycle kind decision, source-signature storage location, disable flag naming, degraded health behavior, and child creation sequencing.
Iteration 8 is lower novelty because it ranks and reconciles prior findings, but the last three completed ratios are not below the 0.05 stop threshold. Continue to iteration 9 synthesis draft.
